text.title: Micro-MVP Cut Rubric Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Micro-MVP Cut Rubric. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Each scope item runs through 5 questions; any fail = cut.
rule.rationale: Each scope item runs through 5 questions; any fail = cut.

rule.statement: Items NOT touching the riskiest assumption test get cut first.
rule.rationale: Items NOT touching the riskiest assumption test get cut first.

rule.statement: Items >3 dev-days get split or cut.
rule.rationale: Items >3 dev-days get split or cut.

rule.statement: Payment, user-management, settings UI are excluded unless they ARE the risk test.
rule.rationale: Payment, user-management, settings UI are excluded unless they ARE the risk test.

rule.statement: Final scope ≤5 dev-days; otherwise re-run the rubric.
rule.rationale: Final scope ≤5 dev-days; otherwise re-run the rubric.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-micro-mvp-cut-rubric.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
